page: Amazon Web Services m6idn.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores based on the x86_64 Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled as a 2850 GB local NVMe SSD instance store, and the network interface delivers a baseline bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities, scoring in the top 25% for Geekbench multi-core and Passmark CPU tests. Image processing and CPU encryption performance also rank in the top 25%. Other workloads, such as single-core stress-ng, Redis SET operations achieving 45.48 million operations per second, and LLM inference processing at 93.17 tokens per second on a medium 7B model, fall within the average tier. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and SHA extensions, which accelerate cryptographic and vector-heavy operations.

The m6idn.12xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency by combining high-speed local NVMe storage with high network throughput, reducing the need for separate high-performance block storage attachments. The balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it appropriate for data-intensive general-purpose workloads. It is suitable for database hosting, static web serving, software compilation, and medium-scale text or image processing tasks.

It includes 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory, a 2850 GB local NVMe SSD instance store, and 75 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers strong multi-core CPU and encryption performance, ranking in the top 25% of benchmarked systems. It supports AVX-512 and SHA hardware acceleration. The balanced resource allocation and high-speed local storage provide qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive applications. This instance is suitable for database operations, software compilation, web serving, and image processing workloads.
